title: the narrative context or design spec that prompted a given content change.
content:
the narrative context or design spec that prompted a given content change.
- **$ORG_ID/tools:** In supporting the OS, there will be various scripts, utilities, or perhaps trained AI models and configurations – these live in the tools repository. For instance, if we develop a custom script to parse the archive indices or a small utility to trigger $AUTOMATION_ENGINE tasks, it resides here. This repo might also hold any custom prompts or code that do not fit in the core (perhaps experiments or one-off tools used by agents). The branch model can be simpler here if needed (maybe mostly main if it’s small, or similar main/dev if tools are complex).
- **$ORG_ID/docs:** Documentation and knowledge intended for human readers or broad consumption lives here. This includes user guides for the OS, narrative documentation (like compiled lorebooks from the worlds), design rationale documents, and possibly exported versions of some archive content for easier browsing. For example, after finalizing this master specification, a cleaned-up version could be placed in the docs repo for reference (while the original lives immutable in the archive). The docs may also contain meta-docs like a contributor guide explaining the branching and naming conventions (thus making this spec actionable for new collaborators). The branch model likely follows main/dev too, but documentation might often be updated directly in main if it’s not sensitive.
context: From document: # Blueprint for a Self-Evolving Creative Operating System — chunk 28 (chunk 28/38)
